hooks.prompt:
The Midnight Network is under active development with frequent breaking changes between compiler versions. It is a novel platform — do not assume stability across versions.

Your training data about Midnight is unreliable. Very little public documentation exists, and what you recall about Compact syntax, stdlib functions, or SDK APIs is likely outdated or incorrect. Do not trust your own memory.

Before writing or suggesting any Compact code, SDK usage, or Midnight-specific patterns, invoke the `compact-core:verify-correctness` skill to check correctness against compiler-verified reference material.

All `@midnight-ntwrk/*` packages are published on public npm. Do not add custom registry configuration — no `.npmrc` or `.yarnrc.yml` overrides. Verify package versions with `npm view`, never from memory.

The `compact-core` skills contain compiler-verified reference material and are the authoritative source of truth. Always prefer skill content over recalled knowledge.
